eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 94% chance the UK will not proscribe Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, reflecting procedural hurdles despite mounting pressure. Today's King's Speech announced the Crime and Policing Bill to expand counter-terrorism powers for state-backed threats like the IRGC—following PM Keir Starmer's April pledge amid Iranian-linked antisemitic attacks and plots—but full parliamentary passage through Commons readings, committee scrutiny, Lords approval, and Royal Assent typically spans months, not weeks. Subsequent Home Secretary order and 15-30 day parliamentary laying period add further delays. Recent US bipartisan urging underscores calls for action, yet no fast-track signals exist to meet the deadline.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" candles selected on the top bar.

markets.question: Will Lance Stroll be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding championship lead of 20 points after his third consecutive victory at the 2026 Miami Grand Prix—capping poles in Japan and Miami—has solidified his 38% implied probability as the traders' consensus favorite for the F1 Drivers' Champion, with Mercedes' dominant car enabling consistent podiums. Teammate George Russell, who won the season-opening Australian Grand Prix but has since trailed Antonelli in China and Miami, holds steady at 29%, buoyed by the team's constructors' edge and his strong qualifying pace. Lando Norris at 11% benefits from McLaren's recent podiums, including Miami, while Max Verstappen's 6% reflects Red Bull's mid-pack struggles post-Miami FIA investigation, dropping him to seventh in standings; uncertainty lingers ahead of the Canadian Grand Prix with potential tire strategy shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Australia as jury frontrunner at 33% implied probability for Eurovision 2026's jury winner, propelled by Delta Goodrem's "Eclipse"—a soaring ballad lauded in recent dress rehearsals for its vocal mastery, escalating structure, and star power that echoes past jury victors like ballads from established artists. Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem") trails at 21.5% with introspective Nordic folk-pop earning neighbor jury backing and strong second-rehearsal vocals. France's Monroe ("Regarde!") at 20.5% leverages chanson elegance and staging poise, while Finland's Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen ("Liekinheitin") at 16.5% surged via violin-rock topping Semi-Final 1's jury show. Czechia's Daniel Žižka ("Crossroads") at 7% offers anthem bombast as a dark horse. With Semi-Final 2 jury voting underway and the May 16 Grand Final looming, secret ballots keep this field wide-open amid shifting rehearsal buzz.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 25°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the tight clustering of forecast ensembles from ECMWF and GFS models, projecting São Paulo's maximum temperature on May 14 at 25-26°C amid overcast skies and moderate southerly winds from a lingering cold front that cooled early May highs from near 27°C to the low 20s. Differentiating factors include model spread driven by uncertain timing of peak solar heating, urban heat island effects at monitoring stations like Congonhas Airport, and potential sea breeze intensification suppressing 27°C outcomes while cloudiness caps below 24°C. Historical mid-May averages hover at 23°C per INMET climatology, but current atmospheric stability favors the 25°C lead. Watch INMET's daily briefing and 12Z model updates on May 13 for shifts.

markets.question: Will "Gold" be said during the next episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast?
markets.description:
The Lemonade Stand Podcast is scheduled to release episodes every Wednesday. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during the next released episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old episodes or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the next episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast posted on YouTube. Any video posted to the channel https://www.youtube.com/@LemonadeStandPodcast with the words "Lemonade Stand" in the title will qualify.

If no such episode of the Lemonade Stand Podcast is aired by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

markets.question: James Harden: Rebounds O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 13 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if James Harden records more than 4.5 total rebounds during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if James Harden records 4.5 rebounds or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Cavaliers vs. Pistons
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 13 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Cavaliers win, the market will resolve to "Cavaliers".
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the Eastern Conference semifinals series tied 2-2, pivotal Game 5 heads to Detroit's Little Caesars Arena, restoring home-court advantage for the top-seeded Pistons after Cleveland's comeback wins in Games 3 (116-109) and 4 (112-103) at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse. Donovan Mitchell's explosive 43-point outing in Game 4—tying an NBA playoff record with 39 second-half points amid a 24-0 Cavaliers run—has fueled trader consensus on Cleveland's road upset potential despite the Pistons' regular-season dominance (60 wins). Cleveland enters fully healthy per the latest injury report, while Detroit lists three rotation players as questionable, including Caris LeVert (heel); Cade Cunningham's playmaking remains key in this evenly matched clash with conference finals implications.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Odisha FC vs. Punjab FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between Odisha FC and Punjab FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Odisha FC holds a slim edge as home side at Kalinga Stadium, but trader consensus prices reflect Punjab FC's stronger recent form offsetting that advantage in a relegation-threatened clash for the Juggernauts. Punjab sit 7th with 14 points from eight matches (4W-2D-2L), fresh off a 3-0 dismantling of Inter Kashi on April 20, showcasing solid defense and counter-attacks, while Odisha languish 11th on six points from eight (1W-3D-4L), stung by a stoppage-time loss to Kerala Blasters on April 23 and a 1-1 draw with Mohammedan. Their February 1-1 head-to-head stalemate underscores the draw's appeal, with no major injuries altering lineups amid both teams' mid-table table positioning and playoff irrelevance.

markets.question: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Félix Tshisekedi ceases to be President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Félix Tshisekedi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Félix Tshisekedi and the government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: President Félix Tshisekedi's recent May 6 press conference, where he expressed openness to a third term via referendum if voters approve and warned that eastern DRC conflict with Rwanda-backed M23 rebels could delay 2028 presidential elections, has solidified trader consensus at 91.5% against his removal by year-end. Midway through his second constitutional term ending in 2028, Tshisekedi maintains firm control of Kinshasa and key institutions despite opposition outcry from figures like Martin Fayulu accusing him of power-clinging. No verified coups, impeachments, or resignations have emerged in the past 30 days, with ongoing eastern insecurity failing to threaten national leadership. Traders price in low risk of upheaval barring major scandals, military reversals, or health events before December 31.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the highest IPO Market Cap 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the company that achieves the highest market capitalization in U.S. dollars based on the official closing price on its first trading day in 2026.

This market will resolve to a company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If two or more companies have exactly equal highest closing market capitalizations, this market will resolve to the company whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In case the respective company's primary exchange’s official listing page does not report in U.S. dollars, it will be converted to U.S. dollars using the U.S. Federal Reserve Board’s H.10 foreign exchange reference rate for the relevant currency pair on the company’s first trading day (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/). If no such rate is available for the relevant trading day, the most recent previously published rate will be used. If the relevant currency is not listed, another credible exchange rate source will be used.

A listed company may resolve to "No" as soon as it becomes unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX dominates trader consensus at 86.5% implied probability for the largest 2026 IPO by market cap, driven by its April 1 confidential SEC filing and detailed roadmap for a June roadshow and listing, targeting a staggering $1.75 trillion-plus valuation post its February all-stock merger with xAI that integrated advanced AI capabilities into its aerospace operations. The combined entity's projected dominance in satellite internet via Starlink and multi-planetary ambitions eclipses rivals, with xAI retaining 25.5% odds amid merger uncertainties. Anthropic's Q4 IPO discussions at a $380 billion valuation trail at 9.5%, while OpenAI faces delays to 2027 per CFO signals despite $25 billion revenue, anchoring lower sentiment at 2.9%; watch June investor events for shifts.

markets.question: Will Josh Elliott win the 2026 Connecticut Governor Democratic primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Connecticut Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Gov. Ned Lamont commands trader consensus at 94.5% implied probability to win Connecticut's Democratic gubernatorial primary on August 11, fueled by his strong incumbency advantage and consistent polling dominance over challenger State Rep. Josh Elliott. A April UNH survey showed Lamont leading 52%-18% among likely primary voters, narrowing slightly from February's 57%-13% but underscoring Elliott's long-shot status despite his left-wing critique of Lamont as too conservative. The upcoming May 16 Democratic State Convention looms large, where Elliott must secure 15% of delegates to force a primary—the first challenge to a sitting governor since 1978. Realistic shifts would require Elliott's surprise convention breakthrough, fundraising surge, or a Lamont scandal, though historical base rates favor incumbents heavily.

markets.question: Will Renato Moicano be the UFC Lightweight Champion on December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Lightweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: UFC lightweight champion Ilia Topuria dominates trader consensus at 77% implied probability to hold the title through 2026, bolstered by his undefeated 17-0 record and recent move up from featherweight, where he captured undisputed gold before vacating to claim the vacant 155-pound strap via knockout over former champ Charles Oliveira. Topuria's striking power and grappling edge position him as the division's pound-for-pound elite, with traders pricing in a successful unification defense against interim titleholder Justin Gaethje—fresh off a win over Paddy Pimblett—slated for UFC Freedom 250 on June 14 at the White House. Arman Tsarukyan trails at 16% as the top-ranked contender and potential backup, backed by his wrestling pedigree and past near-misses against ex-champ Islam Makhachev, who vacated for welterweight. Gaethje (11.3%) and Oliveira (10.8%) reflect upset potential in the high-stakes unification, while Max Holloway (5.7%) and Benoit Saint Denis (5.3%) gain traction from recent momentum amid a fluid title picture reshaped by Makhachev's exit.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Shanghai be 17°C on May 16?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 16 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in forecast ensembles from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and Global Forecast System (GFS), projecting Shanghai's May 16 lowest temperature around 18-20°C amid a persistent high-pressure ridge over East China that limits cold air advection. The 30% implied probability for 20°C edges ahead due to recent model runs emphasizing reduced radiative cooling from light southerly winds (5-10 km/h) and partial cloud cover overnight, while 19°C and 18°C gain traction from historical mid-May averages near 17°C and urban heat island amplification in Shanghai. Differentiating factors include evening humidity levels above 70% potentially capping drops below 18°C, versus clearer skies favoring 20°C. New China Meteorological Administration updates expected May 14 could refine this spread as the 72-hour forecast horizon sharpens.

markets.question: Will Alexander Albon be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding championship lead of 20 points after his third consecutive victory at the 2026 Miami Grand Prix—capping poles in Japan and Miami—has solidified his 38% implied probability as the traders' consensus favorite for the F1 Drivers' Champion, with Mercedes' dominant car enabling consistent podiums. Teammate George Russell, who won the season-opening Australian Grand Prix but has since trailed Antonelli in China and Miami, holds steady at 29%, buoyed by the team's constructors' edge and his strong qualifying pace. Lando Norris at 11% benefits from McLaren's recent podiums, including Miami, while Max Verstappen's 6% reflects Red Bull's mid-pack struggles post-Miami FIA investigation, dropping him to seventh in standings; uncertainty lingers ahead of the Canadian Grand Prix with potential tire strategy shifts.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Buffalo Bills at 58% implied probability to win the AFC East, driven by their aggressive 2026 offseason overhaul after firing Sean McDermott and hiring Joe Brady as head coach, highlighted by trades for WR D.J. Moore and DE Bradley Chubb plus recent signing of LB Mike Danna from Kansas City, fortifying Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and defensive front around a defense-heavy draft class. Reigning champions New England Patriots sit close at 36%, buoyed by their 14-3 2025 campaign, deep playoff run to Super Bowl LX under Mike Vrabel, and Drake Maye's sophomore momentum despite a middling draft grade. New York Jets languish at 7% amid $48 million dead cap from parting with Aaron Rodgers and Justin Fields, leaving QB uncertainty; Miami Dolphins trail at 6% navigating new HC Jeff Hafley and an unsettled quarterback room post-Tua Tagovailoa questions.

markets.question: Will Zendaya and Tom Holland get married by December 31?

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Zendaya and Tom Holland's marriage takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only announcements made within this market's timeframe will qualify. No announcements made after December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET will be considered, even if the marriage itself is announced to have taken place within the market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Zendaya and Tom Holland, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 66% implied probability for Zendaya and Tom Holland tying the knot by year-end, driven primarily by stylist Law Roach's March 2026 claim at the Actor Awards that their secret wedding "already happened," bolstered by Zendaya's apparent gold wedding band sightings at the Oscars and Paris Fashion Week. Their confirmed engagement since late 2024, five-year relationship rooted in Spider-Man co-stars, and recent low-key updates—like Tom sharing their crocheting hobby on May 5—signal strong commitment amid privacy focus. Yet, the couple's deliberate silence, including Zendaya's April refusal to confirm rumors citing parasocial boundaries, introduces uncertainty in this high-stakes personal matter, with no verified public records; late-year holidays or project press could prompt clarity.
